title: Raising geese; killing geese; making feather mattresses; breeding geese
description: Raising geese. They found their own food. Killing geese. Raising ducks. Making feather mattresses. Feathers slipped out a tiny hole in a bag. Attachment to some geese. Doing the whole operation herself. Breeding geese. Swapping for an inferior goose.

title: Father Cataldo stayed with grandfather; conducted services around the country
description: Father Cataldo stayed with her grandfather. They kept house together. He conducted services around the country.

title: Mother lived at DeSmet Mission
description: Her mother lived at DeSmet Mission for several years. Squawmen weren't liked.

title: How father got his land; father lost his leg; helping with work
description: How father got his land. He could have got more land but couldn't handle it. He lost his leg, which made life hard for him. Hired men were sometimes hard to keep. Helping with work.

title: Drummers; train reaching Lewiston
description: Drummers. Train reaching Lewiston killed Genesee.

title: Rosenstein and his children; store in Lapwai
description: Rosenstein had $4,000 on the books which he never collected; he was very kind to people. He was very hard on his son, whom he made run a store in Lapwai. His children were very nice. Mrs. Rosenstein was more Americanized. Dishonest people charged with no intention of paying. Merchants stuck with goods during depressions.

title: Moving location of the town
description: Moving the location of the town when the railroad came.

title: J.P. Vollmer took property away; the wet harvest; muddy roads
description: J.P. Vollmer gave people credit and then took their property away after the wet harvest of 1893. The trademark of Vollmer's holdings - a barn with no house or road. He tried to save his own crop. The wet harvest. Muddy roads.
